title: Why AI gives different answers to the same question

detail.notes: All non-everyday terms (LLM, token, token sampling, probability distribution) defined with plain-language meaning at first appearance. Acronyms spelled out. Concrete analogies (text message autocomplete, cat-on-mat table, bag-of-cards mental image) precede abstractions throughout.

Six-step sampling chain rendered as numbered list (lines 48-53). Temperature comparison rendered as table. Probability distribution rendered as table. All paragraphs within ~4-sentence target. Bold-term definitions used for LLM, token, token sampling, probability distribution.
Reading stays tightly within topic headline. Temperature is prior vocab from 1.3, used without re-teaching. No LLM architecture or training mechanics introduced.

detail.embed_line: ![Token-by-token sampling chain with low vs high temperature contrast](./diagram.png)
detail.caption_text: The token sampling chain: input shapes the probability distribution, the model samples one token, that token is added to the response, and the cycle repeats — with a side panel contrasting how low and high temperature change which tokens get chosen.
